heading: Uniforms of carriers
label: 1730 Uniforms of carriers
text: Whoever, not being connected with the letter-carrier branch of the Postal Service, wears the uniform or badge which may be prescribed by the Postal Service to be worn by letter carriers, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than six months, or both.The provisions of the preceding paragraph shall not apply to an actor or actress in a theatrical, television, or motion-picture production who wears the uniform or badge of the letter-carrier branch of the Postal Service while portraying a member of that service.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 310 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 18735 Stat. 1124Minor verbal change was made.

Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $100” in first par.Pub. L. 103–3221990— struck out “, if the portrayal does not tend to discredit that service” before period at end of second par.Pub. L. 101–6471970— substituted “Postal Service” for “Postmaster General” before “to be worn” in first par.Pub. L. 91–3751968— inserted provision exempting an actor or actress in a theatrical, television, or motion-picture production who wears the uniform or badge of the letter-carrier branch of the Postal Service from the penalties imposed by this section.Pub. L. 90–413

Effective Date of 1970 AmendmentAmendment by effective within 1 year after , on date established therefor by Board of Governors of United States Postal Service and published by it in Federal Register, see , set out as an Effective Date note preceding , Postal Service.Pub. L. 91–375section 15(a) of Pub. L. 91–375section 101 of Title 39Aug. 12, 1970
